43	\section{Setup and motivation}
44	\label{sec:codim1_setup}
45	
46	Fix an algebraically closed field \(\bar k\) throughout. By a \emph{variety} over \(\bar k\)
47	we mean a geometrically integral, separated scheme of finite type over \(\bar k\)
48	(Milne's convention, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, p.~v); a variety is \emph{nonsingular}
49	(or \emph{smooth}) if its local ring at every closed point is regular. A variety is
50	\emph{complete} if it is proper over \(\bar k\).
51	
52	A \emph{rational map} \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) between varieties is the equivalence
53	class of a regular morphism \(\varphi_U \colon U \to Y\) defined on a dense open subset
54	\(U \subseteq X\), where two pairs \((U, \varphi_U)\), \((U', \varphi_{U'})\) are equivalent
55	if \(\varphi_U\) and \(\varphi_{U'}\) agree on \(U \cap U'\) (Milne~\S I.3, p.~16). The map \(f\)
56	is \emph{defined at} a point \(x \in X\) if there is some representative \((U, \varphi_U)\)
57	with \(x \in U\); the set of such \(x\) is an open subset \(\mathrm{Dom}(f) \subseteq X\),
58	the \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\).
59	
60	The motivating question of this chapter, following Milne~\S I.3, is:
61	\begin{quote}
62	  \emph{When does a rational map \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) extend to a regular
63	  morphism on all of \(X\)?}
64	\end{quote}
65	Without hypotheses on \(X\) or \(Y\) the question has no positive answer (Milne lists three
66	counterexamples on p.~16: the inclusion of a proper open into the ambient variety; the
67	rational map to the cuspidal cubic whose inverse fails to extend; the inverse of a
68	blow-up at a smooth point). The two structural inputs that buy unconditional extension
69	in our setting are:
70	\begin{itemize}
71	  \item \emph{Target completeness:} if \(Y\) is complete (proper over \(\bar k\)), the
72	    valuative criterion of properness forces the rational map to extend across every
73	    codim-\(1\) point of a normal source \(X\) (Milne Theorem~3.1).
74	  \item \emph{Target group structure:} if \(Y\) is a group variety, the difference map
75	    \(\Phi(x, y) := f(x) \cdot f(y)^{-1}\) on \(X \times X\) has a vanishing/pole divisor
76	    along the diagonal that controls the codimension of the indeterminacy locus
77	    (Milne Lemma~3.3).
78	\end{itemize}
79	For \(Y\) an abelian variety both conditions hold, and combining them yields Milne's
80	Theorem~3.2 (the headline of \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension}). The
81	present chapter packages the two inputs separately:
82	\cref{thm:codim_one_extension} is Milne~3.1, and
83	\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} is Milne~3.3. The Weil-divisor reformulation
84	\cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} translates the codim-\(1\) extension obstruction
85	in Milne~3.1's proof into the order-at-a-prime-divisor language pinned in
86	\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}, which is the form the Lean prover consumes when
87	working with the project's \(\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}\) API.

178	\section{Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point}
179	\label{sec:smooth_dvr}
180	
181	The codim-\(1\) extension argument in Milne's proof of Theorem~3.1 hinges on the local
182	ring at the generic point of a prime divisor being a discrete valuation ring (so that
183	the valuative criterion of properness applies). On a normal variety this is the
184	defining property of regularity in codimension one; on a smooth variety it is automatic.
185	
186	The smooth-implies-regular-local-stalk implication itself is the Jacobian-criterion
187	direction of Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}; we package it as a separate sub-lemma so that
188	\cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr} below reads cleanly as ``regular local of dimension
189	\(1\) is a DVR'', with the regularity prerequisite supplied by the sub-lemma.
190	
191	\subsection{Stages 5a/5b: K\"ahler-differentials localisation substrate (iter-193)}
192	\label{subsec:kaehler_localisation_substrate}
193	
194	The Jacobian-criterion direction of Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}
195	(\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring} below) factors on the Lean side
196	through a six-stage chain. Stages~1--2 (smooth \(\Rightarrow\) flat at a
197	stalk; smooth \(\Rightarrow\) admits a standard-smooth presentation
198	locally) are axiom-clean since iter-191; Stages~3--4 (the
199	scheme-to-algebra bridge for standard-smooth presentations; freeness of
200	\(\Omega_{S/R}\) for a standard-smooth algebra \(R \to S\)) are
201	axiom-clean since iter-192. The remaining Stages~5--6 reduce the
202	Stacks~00TT conclusion to a freeness plus rank computation on K\"ahler
203	differentials, transported through localisation at the prime defining
204	the stalk. Iter-193 packaged Stages~5a (transport of freeness) and 5b
205	(matching rank under localisation) as the two standalone helpers below;
206	Stage~6 (the remaining Stacks~00OE smooth-algebra dimension formula
207	plus Stacks~02JK cotangent--K\"ahler-over-a-field bridge) is the open
208	Mathlib gap, decomposed iter-198 into the three named sub-lemmas
209	\cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula} (6.A),
210	\cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} (6.B), and
211	\cref{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly} (6.C) in
212	\cref{subsec:stage6_subgap_decomposition} immediately following.
213	
214	\begin{lemma}
215	
216	
217	\leanok
218	  [Stage 5a --- K\"ahler-differentials freeness transports through localisation]
219	  \label{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}
220	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization}
221	  Let \(R\) and \(S\) be commutative rings equipped with an \(R\)-algebra
222	  structure on \(S\), and assume that the module of K\"ahler differentials
223	  \(\Omega_{S/R}\) is free as an \(S\)-module. Let \(M \subseteq S\) be a
224	  submonoid and let \(S_M\) be a localisation of \(S\) at \(M\), so that
225	  \(R \to S \to S_M\) is a tower of \(R\)-algebras. Then the module of
226	  K\"ahler differentials \(\Omega_{S_M / R}\) is free as an
227	  \(S_M\)-module.
228	\end{lemma}

283	\(R \to S\) at a point \(z\), with \(\Omega_{S/R}\) free of rank \(n\)

303	\begin{lemma}
304	  [Stage 6.A --- Smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula (Stacks 00OE)]
305	  \label{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}
306	  \lean{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.ringKrullDim_localization_eq_relativeDimension}
307	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field
308	  % (canonically known as tag 00OE; tag-to-label cross-check not in the
309	  % local `tags/tags` file, see iter-198 writer report note) (read from
310	  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L28206--28217 in section
311	  % "Dimension theory of finitely generated $k$-algebras").
312	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $k$ be a field. Let $S$ be a finite type $k$
313	  % algebra. Let $X = \Spec(S)$. Let $\mathfrak p \subset S$ be a prime
314	  % ideal, and let $x \in X$ be the corresponding point. Then we have
315	  % $\dim_x(X) = \dim(S_{\mathfrak p}) + \text{trdeg}_k\
316	  % \kappa(\mathfrak p)$."
317	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag~00OE (\(=\) \texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}).}
318	  Let \(k\) be a field, let \(S\) be a finite-type \(k\)-algebra, and let
319	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\) be a prime ideal corresponding to a point
320	  \(x \in \mathrm{Spec}(S)\). Then
321	  \[
322	    \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S)
323	    \;=\; \dim S_{\mathfrak q} + \mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q).
324	  \]
325	  In particular, if \(\mathfrak q\) is a maximal ideal (so \(x\) is a
326	  closed point) and \(k\) is algebraically closed, then
327	  \(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = k\) (Nullstellensatz), so
328	  \(\mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q) = 0\) and
329	  \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q} = \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S)\).
330	
331	  \paragraph{Geometric application.} Applied to the iter-193 Stage~3
332	  setup --- \(k = \bar k\), \(S = \Gamma(X.\mathrm{left}, V)\) a
333	  standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra of relative dimension \(n\) (Stage
334	  3), and \(\mathfrak q\) the prime defining the point \(z \in V\) --- the
335	  formula yields \(\dim(\mathcal O_{X, z}) = \dim S_{\mathfrak q} = n -
336	  \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(z)\). For \(z\) a closed point this is
337	  simply \(n\); for the generic point of a codim-\(1\) subvariety
338	  (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) it is \(1\).
339	\end{lemma}
340	
341	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "By Lemma \ref{lemma-dimension-prime-polynomial-ring}
342	% we know that $r = \text{trdeg}_k\ \kappa(\mathfrak p)$ is equal to the
343	% dimension of $V(\mathfrak p)$. Pick any maximal chain of primes
344	% $\mathfrak p \subset \mathfrak p_1 \subset \ldots \subset \mathfrak p_r$
345	% starting with $\mathfrak p$ in $S$. This has length $r$ by Lemma
346	% \ref{lemma-dimension-spell-it-out}. Let $\mathfrak q_j$, $j \in J$ be
347	% the minimal primes of $S$ which are contained in $\mathfrak p$. These
348	% correspond $1-1$ to minimal primes in $S_{\mathfrak p}$ via the rule
349	% $\mathfrak q_j \mapsto \mathfrak q_jS_{\mathfrak p}$. By Lemma
350	% \ref{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-over-field} we know that
351	% $\dim_x(X)$ is equal to the maximum of the dimensions of the rings
352	% $S/\mathfrak q_j$. For each $j$ pick a maximal chain of primes
353	% $\mathfrak q_j \subset \mathfrak p'_1 \subset \ldots \subset
354	% \mathfrak p'_{s(j)} = \mathfrak p$. Then $\dim(S_{\mathfrak p}) =
355	% \max_{j \in J} s(j)$. Now, each chain
356	% $\mathfrak q_j \subset \mathfrak p'_1 \subset \ldots \subset
357	% \mathfrak p'_{s(j)} = \mathfrak p \subset \mathfrak p_1 \subset \ldots
358	% \subset \mathfrak p_r$ is a maximal chain in $S/\mathfrak q_j$, and by
359	% what was said before we have $\dim_x(X) = \max_{j \in J} r + s(j)$.
360	% The lemma follows."
361	\begin{proof}
362	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim}
363	  This is exactly Stacks tag~\texttt{00OE}
364	  (\texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}); the proof is a
365	  citation of the dimension theory of finitely generated algebras over a
366	  field. By Noether normalisation the finite-type \(k\)-algebra \(S\) is
367	  finite over a polynomial subalgebra, so the going-up / catenary
368	  dimension theory of such algebras applies. Write
369	  \(r = \mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\); this transcendence
370	  degree is exactly the dimension of the closed subvariety
371	  \(\overline{\{x\}} = V(\mathfrak q)\). A maximal chain of primes
372	  through \(\mathfrak q\) therefore splits into a
373	  length-\(\dim S_{\mathfrak q}\) part below \(\mathfrak q\) (recording
374	  the local codimension, i.e.\ the height of \(\mathfrak q\) in the
375	  relevant irreducible component) and a length-\(r\) part above it
376	  (recording the dimension of \(\overline{\{x\}}\)). Taking the maximum
377	  over the minimal primes contained in \(\mathfrak q\) yields
378	  \[
379	    \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S) = \dim S_{\mathfrak q} + r
380	    = \dim S_{\mathfrak q} + \mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q),
381	  \]
382	  which is the asserted formula.
383	
384	  For the geometric specialisation matching the Lean target, take
385	  \(k = \bar k\) algebraically closed and \(S\) standard smooth of
386	  relative dimension \(n\), so that \(\dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S) = n\) via
387	  \cref{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim} (the free
388	  K\"ahler module \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q}/R}\) has rank \(n\), which
389	  pins the relative dimension). The formula then gives
390	  \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q} = n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak
391	  q)\). At a closed point \(\mathfrak q\) is maximal, so by the
392	  Nullstellensatz \(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = \bar k\) and
393	  \(\mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q) = 0\); hence
394	  \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q} = \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S) = n\).
395	\end{proof}

397	\begin{lemma}
398	  [Stage 6.B --- Cotangent \(\leftrightarrow\) K\"ahler over a field (Stacks 02JK)]
399	  \label{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
400	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue}
401	  % \lean{...} updated iter-199 review: iter-199 prover landed the
402	  % closed-point iso as a project-local axiom-clean helper (private
403	  % theorem in AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme namespace). Companion siblings:
404	  %   cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
405	  %   finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue

409	  % known as the right-exact conormal portion of tag 02JK; the
410	  % short-exact / split refinement is the proof step inside
411	  % lemma-separable-smooth) (read from
412	  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L34087--34104 and L38724--38766).
413	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "In diagram (\ref{equation-functorial-omega}),
414	  % suppose that $S \to S'$ is surjective with kernel $I \subset S$, and
415	  % assume that $R' = R$. Then there is a canonical exact sequence of
416	  % $S'$-modules
417	  % $$I/I^2 \longrightarrow \Omega_{S/R} \otimes_S S' \longrightarrow
418	  % \Omega_{S'/R} \longrightarrow 0$$
419	  % The leftmost map is characterized by the rule that $f \in I$ maps
420	  % to $\text{d}f \otimes 1$."
421	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Hence we get
422	  % $$\dim_{\kappa} \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa = \dim_\kappa
423	  % \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2 + \text{trdeg}_k (\kappa) \geq \dim R +
424	  % \text{trdeg}_k (\kappa) = \dim_{\mathfrak q} S$$
425	  % (see Lemma \ref{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field} for
426	  % the last equality) with equality if and only if $R$ is regular."
427	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag~02JK (right-exact half = \texttt{lemma-differential-seq}; separable-residue refinement inside \texttt{lemma-separable-smooth}).}
428	  Let \(k\) be a field and let \(R\) be a Noetherian local
429	  \(k\)-algebra with maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\) and residue field
430	  \(\kappa = R/\mathfrak m\). The canonical exact sequence
431	  \[
432	    \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2
433	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa
434	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{\kappa / k}
435	    \;\longrightarrow\; 0
436	  \]
437	  identifies the cotangent space \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) (a
438	  \(\kappa\)-vector space) with the kernel of the right map. When
439	  \(\kappa / k\) is separable (in particular when \(k\) is
440	  algebraically closed and \(\kappa = k\), so \(\Omega_{\kappa / k}
441	  = 0\)) the leftmost map is injective and the sequence is short exact
442	  --- in fact split short exact when there is a \(k\)-algebra section
443	  \(\kappa \to R\) (e.g.\ at a closed point of a \(\bar k\)-variety).
444	  In particular, over an algebraically closed field \(k = \bar k\) and
445	  at a closed point \(\mathfrak m\),
446	  \[
447	    \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa \;\simeq\; \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2
448	  \]
449	  as \(\kappa\)-vector spaces; hence
450	  \(\dim_\kappa(\Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa)
451	   = \dim_\kappa \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\).
452	\end{lemma}

455	  [Stage 6.C --- Assembly: smooth standard-smooth stalk is regular local]
456	  \label{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly}

473	        lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula,
474	        lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
475	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field, let
476	  \(R \to S\) be a standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra presentation of
477	  relative dimension \(n\) on an affine chart \(\Spec S \subseteq X\),
478	  and let \(z \in \Spec S\) be a point corresponding to a prime
479	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\). Then the stalk \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) is a

483	  \(\mathrm{trdeg} = 0\)) the dimension equals \(n\); in the

489	  Combine the three sub-lemmas:
490	  \begin{enumerate}
491	    \item By \cref{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim} (Stage~5b)
492	      the localised K\"ahler module \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q} / R}\)

495	      Stacks 00OE) the Krull dimension of \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) equals

497	      which for a standard-smooth presentation of relative dimension

501	      structure: \(n\) variables modulo \(n - \dim\) equations, see

503	    \item By \cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} (Stage 6.B,

513	      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\); the

702	form is not available.

140	`Z(f) := X ∖ Dom(f) ⊆ X`.

142	`f` is everywhere defined (i.e. extends to a regular morphism) iff

145	Blueprint reference: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
146	§I.3, p. 16). -/
147	def indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Set X :=
148	  (f.domain : Set X)ᶜ
149	
150	/-- The indeterminacy locus of a rational map is closed (it is the complement
151	of an open). -/
152	lemma isClosed_indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
153	    IsClosed (indeterminacyLocus f) := by
154	  unfold indeterminacyLocus
155	  exact (f.domain.isOpen).isClosed_compl
156	
157	/-! ## §2. Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps
158	
159	A rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is **codim-1-indeterminacy-free** if its
160	indeterminacy locus contains no prime divisor of `X`, i.e. every codim-1
161	generic point of `X` already lies in `f.domain`. Equivalently (under the
162	project's standing `Order.coheight` convention on the specialisation
163	preorder of `X.carrier`), every `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in
164	`f.domain`.
165	
166	Blueprint pin: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1, the
167	conclusion). -/
168	
169	/-- **Codim-1-indeterminacy-free rational map.** A rational map
170	`f : X ⇢ Y` is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if every codimension-one point
171	of `X` lies in its domain of definition. Equivalently: no prime divisor of
172	`X` (i.e. closure of a codim-1 generic point) is contained in
173	`indeterminacyLocus f`.
174	
175	The codim-1 condition is encoded via `Order.coheight η = 1` on the
176	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier`, matching the project's standing
177	convention in `Scheme.PrimeDivisor` (`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`).
178	
179	Blueprint reference: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
180	Varieties*, Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
181	def CodimOneFree {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Prop :=
182	  ∀ (x : X), Order.coheight x = 1 → x ∈ f.domain
183	
184	end RationalMap
185	
186	/-! ## §3. Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point
187	
188	On a nonsingular variety `X` over `k̄`, the local ring at the generic point
189	of a prime divisor is a discrete valuation ring with fraction field equal to
190	the function field `K(X) = X.functionField`. This is Hartshorne II.6's
191	"regular in codimension one" property combined with the regular-local-ring-of-dim-1
192	$\Leftrightarrow$ DVR equivalence (Atiyah–Macdonald 9.2; Stacks `00PD`).
193
